mentions.note: Heavy AI capex spooked the stock, but dominant ad business and Zuckerberg's execution keep Cramer positive.
Social media strength visible in Taiwan Semi transcript; benefiting from hyperscaler AI capex validation and demand confirmation.
Down 8% YTD; mega-cap tech underperformance vs. speculative names suggests rotation away from quality.
Down 8% YTD as money rotates out of quality mega-cap tech into speculative froth; high-quality company but losing to riskier bets.
P/E multiple contracted from 30x to 22x earnings; market skeptical of growth rate; Cramer says Meta has 'lost its way' amid OpenAI competition and generative AI platform threats.
Terrible until today; nice move yesterday, recommended last 2 days. Mark Zuckerberg lowered boom on spending all capex can; explain how making money on data center buildout beyond smart glasses.

Trading <23x earnings after 16% decline from August highs. Strong ad numbers won't matter if 2026 CapEx guidance disappoints again. Catalyst needed on CapEx or AI ROI clarity. Watch Wednesday earnings.

Reports Wednesday. Core ad business strong but CapEx guidance will dominate reaction — 2026 spend forecast even higher than 2025. Need clarity on ROI from multi-billion-dollar AI investments. Stock down 16% from August highs; catalyst required for rally.
Massive earnings and revenue beat; confident can grow operating income YoY despite $135B AI spend; rallying furiously.
AI-powered advertising with 3.5B daily users now proven force multiplier; Mark Zuckerberg confirmed AI is greatest competitive advantage; not done going higher.
Terrific quarter but market narrative stuck on advertising company with Ray-Ban glasses; company not defending or promoting stock; frustratingly indifferent.
Beat top and bottom; Zuckerberg explained how AI already improving ad profitability; sold off 5 of 6 days but quarter now free.
Caller Robert (CA) asked about volatility; Cramer: could embrace limited AI-driven restructuring (like Block), cut staff, boost shareholder value; potential inflection model.
Lost court case on social media as new tobacco; stock lost tens of billions interim but EPS impact ultimately minimal; recovery model like J&J.
Two state court losses ($375M + $6M) look existential but weak on merit; federal appeals likely to overturn (no jury, Section 230 defense strong); J&J talc case precedent supports recovery.
Trading at 19x earnings despite legal losses; appeals likely and First Amendment protections should trim awards. Multiple compression overdone; patient buyers rewarded post-appeal clarity.
Data center buildout essential; okay showing today but rally needed broader leadership.
Hardware user and beneficiary of AI capex wave; part of winning FANG-style camp alongside Amazon and Alphabet.
Surged 4.4% on announcement of new AI model Llama; talent spending finally paying off. Earlier fears about wasted AI investment dissipated.
Meta is back; beneficiary of chip demand and infrastructure investment; rotation leader after correction.
100X data shows Meta lagging in AI future usage intent but starting to improve; watch for competitive progress.
Fourth-layer AI model (Meta Superintelligence); fifth-layer chatbot infrastructure; AI infrastructure and model player.
10% layoff announcement foolish market reaction; Zuckerberg is 'chainsaw' who cuts costs ruthlessly when needed—stock should have gone higher, not lower.
Mega-cap tech earnings tomorrow; serial underperformer in recent quarters; watch for capex discipline. Held in trust.
Raised capex by $10B despite 33% revenue growth—red flag; fastest revenue growth in 5 years but CapEx hike without clear justification concerning; don't buy.

33% revenue growth (best in 5 years), 62% EPS growth, but family DAP shrank vs prior quarter, CapEx raised $10B to $125–145B for component inflation; lacks cloud infrastructure monetization path like peers; investors unconvinced on AI spending justification; stock down 8%.
Fourth-largest US IPO (Facebook); cut in half first few months (mobile transition problem), but became incredible long-term stock within months of IPO. Patience paid off.
No cloud business to offset losses from AI initiatives away from Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp; traditional business strength not offsetting.
33% Q growth, fastest 5 yrs; $56B revenue, 3.5B daily active users, $150B cap ex; Mark Zuckerberg innovative; buy on pullback after morning lows.
Major model builder spending insane amounts on AI infrastructure; part of AI revolution layer 4.
Working well with AI capabilities; benefits from AI-driven data center and platform optimization.
Powerful product suite (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, AI, eyeglasses) but stalled momentum; 'what have you done lately' question unanswered; printing money profile weakened.

Leading AR/VR wearables race with best-in-class hardware. Meta's programs teaching small/medium businesses trades (electricians, plumbers, data center technicians) demonstrate smart capital deployment for future workforce development; can't salute them enough.
Down 11% in June; viewed as pure ad play with global exposure; needs to announce Meta Web Services (cloud) business to unlock 100+ point gain.
Announced cloud infrastructure business as instantly profitable enterprise play; rallied ~$50 today, potential $100+ more as pricing power expands beyond consumer advertising.
Hyperscaler bouncing on rotation back to Mag 7 after hardware selloff. Defying potential lawsuit/tobacco-like liability concerns as sentiment shifts. Part of 'walking dead' that got fresh money.
Initial panic on Broadcom chip production and capex ramp reversed to a $28 gain; Zuckerberg likely has a high-ROI monetization plan across 3.5B users and WhatsApp.
Confirmed plan to monetize excess compute capacity, delivering nearly 100 points this month; exemplifies big tech's ability to create its own catalysts.
Called 'rotten fruit'—still coasting on merchant-of-computing narrative from week ago; unclear if rally justified by fundamentals.
